product.one_line_description: Netlify provides a REST API to manage atomic deploys of sites and apps, form submissions, JavaScript snippets, DNS, and more.

authentication.auth_summary: Netlify uses OAuth2 for authentication. Users can generate Personal Access Tokens (PATs) for manual authentication in scripts or commands, which are included as Bearer tokens in the Authorization header. For public integrations, OAuth2 client keys and secrets are used, requiring registration of a new application.

developer_access.access_notes: Personal Access Tokens (PATs) can be generated by users from their Netlify account settings under 'Applications > Personal access tokens'. OAuth2 applications can be registered in user settings under 'OAuth applications' to obtain client keys and secrets.

api_surface.api_summary: Netlify offers a comprehensive REST API for managing various aspects of web projects, including atomic deploys, site creation/updates/deletion, file management, environment variables, forms (metadata, submissions, spam), and webhooks. It also provides Go and JS client SDKs for convenience.

buildability.verdict_reasoning: Self-serve developer access is verified with a broad/moderate documented public API.

evidence_pool.claim: Netlify uses OAuth2 for authentication. Users can generate Personal Access Tokens (PATs) for manual authentication in scripts or commands, which are included as Bearer tokens in the Authorization header. For public integrations, OAuth2 client keys and secrets are used, requiring registration of a new application.
evidence_pool.source_title: Get started with the Netlify API | Netlify Docs
evidence_pool.evidence_snippet: Netlify uses OAuth2 for authentication. All requests must use HTTPS. To generate a personal access token (PAT): ... Use your PAT for manual authentication in shell scripts or commands that use the Netlify API. To authenticate API requests, include the token in the authorization header: `Authorization: Bearer <YOUR_PERSONAL_ACCESS_TOKEN>`. ... If you’re making a public integration with Netlify for others to enjoy, you must use OAuth2. ... You’ll need an application client key and a client secret to integrate with the Netlify API. You can register a new application in your Netlify user settings for [OAuth applications](https://app.netlify.com/applications).

evidence_pool.source_title: Get started with the Netlify API | Netlify Docs
evidence_pool.evidence_snippet: To generate a personal access token (PAT): 1. Go to [**Applications \> Personal access tokens**](https://app.netlify.com/user/applications#personal-access-tokens). 2. Select **New access token**.

evidence_pool.source_title: Get started with the Netlify API | Netlify Docs
evidence_pool.evidence_snippet: To generate a personal access token (PAT): 1. Go to [**Applications \> Personal access tokens**](https://app.netlify.com/user/applications#personal-access-tokens). 2. Select **New access token**. ... You can register a new application in your Netlify user settings for [OAuth applications](https://app.netlify.com/applications).

evidence_pool.evidence_snippet: Netlify can trigger webhooks, send email notifications, or send Slack messages on certain events. The `/hooks` endpoint lets you control the hooks for your site.

evidence_pool.source_title: Get started with the Netlify API | Netlify Docs
evidence_pool.evidence_snippet: Use the Netlify REST API to manage atomic deploys of your sites and apps, manage form submissions, inject JavaScript snippets, manage DNS, and so much more. This document covers the basics for interacting with the Netlify API, plus instructions for [deploying sites](/api-and-cli-guides/api-guides/get-started-with-api#deploy-with-the-api) and notes on some [commonly used endpoints](/api-and-cli-guides/api-guides/get-started-with-api#commonly-used-endpoints).

evidence_pool.claim: Netlify offers a comprehensive REST API for managing various aspects of web projects, including atomic deploys, site creation/updates/deletion, file management, environment variables, forms (metadata, submissions, spam), and webhooks. It also provides Go and JS client SDKs for convenience.
evidence_pool.source_title: Get started with the Netlify API | Netlify Docs
evidence_pool.evidence_snippet: Use the Netlify REST API to manage atomic deploys of your sites and apps, manage form submissions, inject JavaScript snippets, manage DNS, and so much more. This document covers the basics for interacting with the Netlify API, plus instructions for [deploying sites](/api-and-cli-guides/api-guides/get-started-with-api#deploy-with-the-api) and notes on some [commonly used endpoints](/api-and-cli-guides/api-guides/get-started-with-api#commonly-used-endpoints). Additionally, we have two API clients for your convenience: Go Client, JS Client. ... The `/sites` endpoint allows you to access sites deployed on Netlify. ... The [environment variables API endpoints](https://open-api.netlify.com/#tag/environmentVariables) allow you to access and set both site and shared environment variables. ... All files deployed by Netlify can be read through the API. ... You can access all deploys for a specific site. ... You can access all [Netlify Forms](/manage/forms/setup) metadata and submissions for a site. ... The `/hooks` endpoint lets you control the hooks for your site.

evidence_pool.source_title: Get started with the Netlify API | Netlify Docs
